title: The Gaelic Athlete, vol. VIII, no. 197, Saturday October 9th 1915

October 9, 1916

Athletics & Cycling

HARP C.C. SPORTS

INTERESTING CYCLING AT CROKE PARK

The Emlit Championship of Ireland. In a bad year for things sporting, the Harp C.C. have made two ambitious and entertaining attempts to put Dublin with a high-class sports fixture. Their initial effort took place in August, when the promoting body experienced the hardest of hard luck in clashing with the O'Donovan Rossa funeral. Their second attempt was made on Sunday last before a moderate attendance, in seasonable weather. The 'gate' was not so good as might reasonably be expected, and Mr. P. O'Connell (Arbuckle) as hand-capper and starter, gave all-round satisfaction. Details:

100 yards - J. J. O'Halloran (Limerick), 1. J. E. Harris (Limerick), 2. 200 yards - J. J. O'Halloran (Limerick), 1. J. E. Harris (Limerick), 2. 300 yards - J. J. O'Halloran (Limerick), 1. J. E.
